pages.title: About Me
pages.text: I am a PhD student in the Department of Computer Science, University of Warwick. I work under the supervision of Prof. Chang-Tsun Li. Research InterestMy research interests include computer vision, deep learning, and transfer learning, and particularly person matching and recognition in video surveillance system (Person Re-Identification). Research Experience Research Assistant | Nanyang Technological University | Oct 2017 - Present Research Assistant | Hong Kong Baptist University | Dec 2016 - Mar 2017 Publication Shan Lin, Chang-Tsun Li. Person Re-identification with Soft Biometrics through Deep Learning. In Deep Biometrics, Springer, 2019. [PDF] Yihan Guo, Shan Lin, Xiao Ma, Jay Bal, Chang-Tsun Li. Homogeneous Feature Transfer and Heterogeneous Location Fine-tuning for Cross-City Property Appraisal Framework. In Australasian Data Mining Conference (AusDM), 2018. [PDF] [PPT] Shan Lin, Haoliang Li, Chang-Tsun Li, Alex C Kot. Multi-task Mid-level Feature Alignment Network for Unsupervised Cross-Dataset Person Re-Identification. In British Machine Vision Conference (BMVC), 2018. (spotlights, 2.3% acceptance rate) [PDF][PPT] Shan Lin, Chang-Tsun Li. End-to-End Correspondence and Relationship Learning of Mid-Level Deep Features for Person Re-Identification. In International Conference on Digital Image Computing: Techniques and Applications (DICTA), 2017. [PDF] [Poster]

posts.title: Caffe Guide
posts.text: List some Caffe installation problems. Caffe HDF5 ProblemChanges in Makefile.config file 1INCLUDE_DIRS := $(PYTHON_INCLUDE) /usr/local/include /usr/include/hdf5/serial/ 2LIBRARY_DIRS := $(PYTHON_LIB) /usr/local/lib /usr/lib /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/hdf5/serial/ Caffe Boost Python3 ProblemChanged boost_python3 in the file Makefile.config to boost_python-py35 Caffe cuBlas Errorsudo rm -rf .nv/ Mac Caffe Openblas1BLAS_INCLUDE := $(shell brew --prefix openblas)/include2BLAS_LIB := $(shell brew --prefix openblas)/lib

posts.title: Chrome Guide
posts.text: List Some Tips and Tricks for Chrome Quick Switch Different Search Enging in Address Bar Enter Chrome Setting: chrome://settings/ Edit Mange Search Engines Change the Keyword for the search engins you like to use: g: google.com b: baidu.com y: youtube.com z: zhihu.com In Chrome address bar, g+(space/tab) will be google search, y+(space/tab) will be youtube search Vimium ExtentionUse vim shortcuts control chrome webpage Install from Chrome Web Store [Link]Common Shortcuts j,k up, down h,l left, right u,d half page up, down r refresh H,L page forward, backward [Full Shortcut List] TampermonkeyTampermonkey is a userscript manager for browser Install from Chrome Web Store [Link] Scripts can be found and installed from Greasy Fork 解除B站区域限制 百度网盘直接下载助手 Add Google Calendar Back to chrome://apps PageChrome App page no longer has official Google Calendar App. The following method can create a URL link APP in the Chrome App page. This method can also be used for other website URL as well. Navigate to https://calendar.google.com/calendar/r Press F12 to bring up the Developer Tools Panel. Click the Console tab. Google an icon of your choosing by typing “Google Calendar.ico” into Google Images. I’ll be using this one for this example:(https://png.icons8.com/color/1600/google-calendar.png)[https://png.icons8.com/color/1600/google-calendar.png] Paste this code in, replacing the “link.href” part with your chosen icon and press enter: 1(function() {2 var link = document.querySelector("link[rel*='icon']") || document.createElement('link');3 link.type = 'image/x-icon';4 link.rel = 'shortcut icon';5 link.href = 'https://png.icons8.com/color/1600/google-calendar.png';6 document.getElementsByTagName('head')[0].appendChild(link);7})(); You’ll notice the web page’s icon will change. Click the sandwich menu others have pointed out, and click More tools.. > Create shortcut. Call it “Google Calendar”. Go to chrome://apps or click the Apps button in the top-left of your browser and you should see the new high quality icon there. You can fell free delete the shortcut file just create in your PC or Mac.

posts.title: Git Guide
posts.text: Some Git tips Git Cache Github Credential in Linux/MacTurn on the credential helper so that Git will save your password in memory for some time. By default, Git will cache your password for 15 minutes.1# Set git to use the credential memory cache2git config --global credential.helper cache To change the default password cache timeout:1# Set the cache to timeout after 1 hour (setting is in seconds)2git config --global credential.helper 'cache --timeout=3600' Git Cache Github Credential in WindowsIn Windows system, you need The Git Credential Manager for Windows (GCM) for Caching Github Credential. The GCM provides secure Git credential storage for Windows. [Github] [Lastest Installer]

posts.title: Command Line Guide
posts.text: Command Line tips and tricks A Simplified Collection of man Pages: tldrMacOS Install: 1brew install tldr Ubuntu Install: 1npm install -g tldr Disk Usage Analyzer: ncduncdu provide a interactive user interface. It can quickly scan and display the disk usage of folders and files. MacOS Install: 1brew install ncdu Ubuntu Install: 1sudo apt install ncdu Run a Command or Shell-Script in Background: nohupWhen you execute a Unix job when ssh to a server, and logout from the session, your process will get killed. You can avoid this using by executing the job with nohup 1nohup command-with-options &23## simple example4nohup python train.py &5nohup sh train.sh &67## give a different output file name8nohup python train.py &> nohuo2.out & Out-of-Box Vim Config: Space VimInstall 1curl -sLf https://spacevim.org/install.sh | bash Process Viewer: htop / gtophtop is the best an command line interactive process viewer. 1# MacOS install htop2brew install htop34# Ubuntu install htop5sudo apt install htop gtop is the just a show off (require node.js). Github 1npm install gtop -g lazygitTerminal UI for git commands. Github Tutorial MacOS Install1brew tap jesseduffield/lazygit2brew install lazygit Ubuntu Install1sudo add-apt-repository ppa:lazygit-team/release2sudo apt-get update3sudo apt-get install lazygit45# Conda install6conda install -c conda-forge lazygit

posts.title: Set Up Hexo Blog in GitHub Pages
posts.text: Set Up and Deploy Hexo Blog to Github Pages Github PagesCreate a new repository named username.github.io, where username is your username (or organization name) on GitHub. Hexo install Node.js install Git (Windows) install Hexo npm install -g hexo-cli create a local blog create a blog folder in the blog folder, run hexo init to initialize the hexo blog run npm install to install all the dependencies hexo g will generate the static webpage hexo s will run the server in http://localhost:4000/ create a new post hexo new 'My First Post' Deploy the Hexo blog to Github Pages install hexo-deployer-git npm install hexo-deployer-git --save Then modify the property deploy of the file _config.yml1deploy: 2 type: git 3 repository: https://github.com/LinShanify/LinShanify.github.io.git 4 branch: master push the hexo blog to github pages repository: hexo d Change ThemeThe theme I used for my personal homepage is minos. Install theme: 1git clone https://github.com/ppoffice/hexo-theme-minos.git themes/minos Then modify the property theme of the file hexo/_config.yml to theme: minos. The detail installation and moditication guide can be found here.

posts.title: Jupyter Guide
posts.text: Jupyter Tips and Tricks Set Up Password: Link1jupyter notebook --generate-config2jupyter notebook password Magic Commands: %matplotlib inline: inline graph plot1%matplotlib inline2%config InlineBackend.figure_format = 'svg'3plt.savefig('tmp.eps', bbox_inches='tight') %load: insert the code from an external script %load ./hello_world.py %%writefile:save the contents of that cell to an external file %%writefile hello_world.py !:Executing Shell Commands !pip install numpy %%time: Time information about a single run of the code in your cell. Jupyter Widgets: with pip 1pip install ipywidgets2jupyter nbextension enable --py widgetsnbextension with condaconda install -c conda-forge ipywidgets Additional For Jupyter Lab: install node.js run jupyter labextension install @jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager Jupyter Extension Manager:A JupyterLab extension to facilitate the discovery and installation of other extensions. Go into advanced settings editor. Open the Extension Manager section. Add the entry “enabled”: true. Save the settings.

posts.title: MacOS Guide
posts.text: Some useful MacOS tools MacOS Best Productive Tool: AlfredMacOS上 Best Productive Tool Website MacOS Best Windows Splitter: MagnetInstall: Mac App Store MacOS Reverse Mouse Scroll: Scroll ReverserMacOS follow natural scroll direction, but it will change mouse scroll direction as well. Scroll Reversercan reverse the mouse scroll direction without affecting the trackpacd. Download MacOS Best Video Converter: HandBrake (Open Sources)Download / Github MacOS QuickLook plugin for WebP image filesWebP is Google’s new image format and OS X doesn’t recognize the .webp files. This plugin will give you an ability to see previews and thumbnails of WebP images. github Copy paste and run the following command on the terminal app: 1curl -L https://raw.github.com/emin/WebPQuickLook/master/install.sh | sh

posts.title: Python Automation
posts.text: Some python automation tools Python Job Scheduling: scheduleschedule lets you run Python functions periodically at pre-determined intervals using a simple, human-friendly syntax. [Github] Install pip install schedule Usage:1import schedule2import time34def job():5 print("I'm working...")67schedule.every(10).minutes.do(job)8schedule.every().hour.do(job)9schedule.every().day.at("10:30").do(job)10schedule.every(5).to(10).minutes.do(job)11schedule.every().monday.do(job)12schedule.every().wednesday.at("13:15").do(job)13schedule.every().minute.at(":17").do(job)1415while True:16 schedule.run_pending()17 time.sleep(1) Python Browser Automation: seleniumselenium lets you automating web applications for testing purposes or scraping. [Website] Install pip install selenium Download corresponding webdriver and unzip to current folder. [Chrome] Normal Usage:1from selenium import webdriver2browser = webdriver.Chrome(executable_path='./chromedriver') 34## Open Webpage5browser.get('https://ntu-reid.com/user/sign-in')67## Fill Up Email Field8email = browser.find_element_by_id('email')9email.send_keys('username@gmail.com')1011## Press Button12submit = browser.find_element_by_class_name('btn')13submit.click()1415## Close Browser16browser.quit() Server Usage (Headless mode and Allow File Download):1from selenium import webdriver23## Allow File Download in Headless Mode4def enable_downoad_headless(browser,download_dir):5 browser.command_executor._commands["send_command"] = ("POST", '/session/$sessionId/chromium/send_command')6 params = {'cmd':'Page.setDownloadBehavior', 'params': {'behavior': 'allow', 'downloadPath': download_dir}}7 browser.execute("send_command", params)89## Headless Chrome Setting for Server10chrome_options = webdriver.ChromeOptions()11chrome_options.add_argument('--headless')12chrome_options.add_argument('--disable-gpu')13chrome_options.add_argument('--no-sandbox')1415browser = webdriver.Chrome(options=chrome_options, executable_path='./chromedriver') 16## Allow Browser Download File into Path17enable_downoad_headless(browser, path) Python GUI Automation: PyAutoGUIPyAutoGUI is a cross-platform GUI automation Python module for human beings. Used to programmatically control the mouse & keyboard. [Github]

posts.title: SSH Guide
posts.text: Set Up SSH Connect to a remote server using a specific port:1ssh username@remote_host -p 2222 SSH tunneling: port forwarding1ssh username@remote_host -L 8888:127.0.0.1:8888 Passwordless Generate Your Key (optional) 1$ ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 Copy Key to SSH server 1$ ssh-copy-id username@remote_host -p 2222

posts.title: Manual Setup Shadowsocks Server
posts.text: Manual Set Up ShadowSocks VPN Server Install ShadowSocks ServerInstall Python Version of ShadowSocks 1# install pip2sudo apt-get install python-pip34# install shadowsocks5sudo pip install shadowsocks Setup ShadowSocks Config FileCreate a json config file 1sudo nano /etc/shadowsocks.json Change the following config with your server ip address and password. Copy to the shadowsocks.json file just created. 1{2 "server":"your_server_ip",3 "server_port":8388,4 "local_address": "127.0.0.1",5 "local_port":1080,6 "password":"yourpassword",7 "timeout":300,8 "method":"aes-256-cfb",9 "fast_open": false10} Start ShadowSocks Server1# running the ss server2ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json34# running the ss server in the background5ssserver -c /etc/shadowsocks.json -d start Fix EVP_CIPHER_CTX_cleanup errorwhen openssl version is 1.1.0 and above, starting shadowsocks2.8.2 server will show undefined symbol: EVP_CIPHER_CTX_cleanup error. because in openssl1.1.0，they abandon EVP_CIPHER_CTX_cleanup function and change to EVP_CIPHER_CTX_reset. Solution: edit ShaodowSocks openssl.py file1vim /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/shadowsocks/crypto/openssl.py go to line 52（shadowsocks2.8.2 version，other version search cleanup）1# Change2libcrypto.EVP_CIPHER_CTX_cleanup.argtypes = (c_void_p,)34# TO5libcrypto.EVP_CIPHER_CTX_reset.argtypes = (c_void_p,) go to line 111 (shadowsocks2.8.2 version，other version search cleanup）1# Change2libcrypto.EVP_CIPHER_CTX_cleanup(self._ctx) 34# TO5改为libcrypto.EVP_CIPHER_CTX_reset(self._ctx) Best ShadowSocks Client: OutlineDesktop:Windows / MacOS / Linux Mobile: iOS / Android Generating Access Key: Outline Access Key Formate: ss://method:password@hostname:portExample: 1ss://aes-256-cfb:yourpassword@your_server_ip:8388 Then you need to BASE64 encode this URI. You can encode and generate the QR Code on the ShadowSocks Website here

posts.title: Shadowsocks All in One Script Installation
posts.text: Shadowsocks Python Version All in One Script Installation Default Config Server Port：default 8989 Password：default abc123456 Encryption Method：default aes-256-gcm Note：default config setting is for a single user. If you want multi-user setup, follow the instruction of editing config file below. Script Installation1# Download Script2wget --no-check-certificate https://raw.githubusercontent.com/quniu/shadowsocks-all/master/install/shadowsocks-all.sh34# If anythong wrong with the download,5# Download from my Gist backup6wget https://gist.githubusercontent.com/LinShanify/060862fdab7d3e6369769daaffd8b91f/raw/32f3764a66fdb0ff69f02ffb6386ffcb7001295c/shadowsocks-all.sh78# Change Permission9sudo chmod +x shadowsocks-all.sh1011# Installation12sudo ./shadowsocks-all.sh 2>&1 | tee shadowsocks.log Follow the instruction. Once completed, you will have a similar terminal output shown below. 1Starting Shadowsocks success!23Congratulations, Shadowsocks-Python server install completed!4Your Server IP : 35.147.250.111 5Your Server Port : 8989 6Your Password : abc123456 7Your Encryption Method: aes-256-gcm 89Your QR Code: (For Shadowsocks Windows, OSX, Android and iOS clients)10 ss://YWVzLTI1Ni1nY206YWJjMTIzNDU2QDM1LjE4Ny4yMjkuMTI4Ojg5ODk= 11Your QR Code has been saved as a PNG file path:12 /root/shadowsocks_python_qr.png 1314Installation has been completed, enjoy it! Uninstall： 1sudo ./shadowsocks-all.sh uninstall Check Service StatusAfter successful installation ， shadowsocks-python serveris should be running in the background. you can check shadowsocks-python status by running: 1service shadowsocks-python status Note：the script installation ins already make the shaodowsocks-python autorun on boot. Edit Config File.Config File Path：/etc/shadowsocks-python/config.json 1{2 "server":"0.0.0.0",3 "server_port":8989,4 "local_address":"127.0.0.1",5 "local_port":1080,6 "password":"abc123456",7 "timeout":300,8 "method":"aes-256-gcm",9 "fast_open":true10} Multi-User Multi-Port Sample 1{2 "server":"0.0.0.0",3 "local_address":"127.0.0.1",4 "local_port":1080,5 "port_password":{6 "8989":"password0",7 "9001":"password1",8 "9002":"password2",9 "9003":"password3",10 "9004":"password4"11 },12 "timeout":300,13 "method":"aes-256-gcm",14 "fast_open": false15} Command1# start | stop | restart | status23# Restart4sudo service shadowsocks-python restart5# Stop6sudo service shadowsocks-python stop7# Start8sudo service shadowsocks-python start9# Check Status10sudo service shadowsocks-python status1112# Restart the Shadowsocks13/etc/init.d/shadowsocks-python restart

posts.title: Ubuntu Guide
posts.text: Ubuntu Tips and Tricks Bootable Ubuntu USB Stick Tools Download the ubuntu desktop ISO file from here Download the Rufus, a free and open source USB stick writing tool Follow the instruction in Rufus software, the detail installation guid can be found here Fix Ubuntu-Windows Dual Boot Time ConflictThe Ubuntu-Windows DualBoot will always change the Windows time to UTC after rebooting from Ubuntu. We can fix the time conflict by running the following command in Ubuntu terminal. 1timedatectl set-local-rtc 1 --adjust-system-clock Change GRUB Default Boot OrderThe Ubuntu-Windows DualBoot will set the Ubuntu as the default operating system to boot. We can change the default boot to Windows by modifying the property GRUB_DEFAULT=4 in the ubuntu default grub file. (the number may vary) 1sudo nano /etc/default/grub2sudo update-grub Ubuntu Create New Sudo User Use the adduser command to add a new user to your system.1sudo adduser <username> Use the usermod command to add the user to the sudo group.1usermod -aG sudo <username> Change Hostname (Computer Name)Edit/etc/hostnameand/etc/hoststwo files. 1sudo gedit /etc/hostname /etc/hosts Ubuntu Pinyin For Ubuntu 16.04 uses fcitx google pinyin1sudo apt-get install fcitx-googlepinyin For Ubuntu 18.05 has to use ibus1sudo apt-get install ibus-pinyin2sudo apt-get install ibus-sunpinyin3ibus restart Source .bashrc at SSH Login.bashrc is not sourced when you log in using SSH. You need to source it in your .bash_profile like this: 1if [ -f ~/.bashrc ]; then2 . ~/.bashrc3fi A login shell first reads /etc/profile and then ~/.bash_profile. A non-login shell reads from /etc/bash.bashrc and then ~/.bashrc Restore .bashrcIn case, the .bashrc been accidentally deleted you can run following command to restore the default .bashrc. 1/bin/cp /etc/skel/.bashrc ~/2source ~/.bashrc Ubuntu Shutdown and Restart Shut Down: sudo poweroff Restart: sudo reboot Fix Chrome Opens Black Window for URLgoogle-chrome.desktop is missing the %U argument Open file:$HOME/.local/share/applications/google-chrome.desktop Find the line:Exec=/opt/google/chrome/chrome Add a space and %U: Exec=/opt/google/chrome/chrome %U Ubuntu Change Time Zone in Commnad Line1sudo dpkg-reconfigure tzdata Localhost IPlocalhost = 127.0.0.1 Ubuntu Labtop Lid Close Ignore in terminal do”sudo nano /etc/UPower/UPower.cong Change IgnoreLid to IgnoreLid=true Save and exit the editor Restart the UPower services with:sudo service upower restart

posts.text: VS Code Tips and Tricks Must Have ExtentionSync VS Code Setting Between Computers: Settings SyncUse GitHub Gist to Sync the VS Code Setting.Shortcuts Upload Key : Shift + Alt + U Download Key : Shift + Alt + D Detail Instruction is [here]
